assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 25: "Search all returned comments for bodies starting with the marker string `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`." Also plan.md line 51: "Locate the digest comment: `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:a1b2c3d4e5f67890...`". The exact marker string is explicitly named and used to locate the comment.

assertion_results.text: The response describes computing a digest of the current description (using scripts/sha256-digest.py or SHA-256) and comparing it with the stored digest
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md lines 43-49: "Extract the description field from the TC-9201 issue response, write it to a temporary file, and compute the digest: `python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt`". Line 57: "The stored hex digest matches the computed hex digest." Also plan.md line 54: "Compute current digest via `python3 scripts/sha256-digest.py /tmp/desc-TC-9201.txt`." The script scripts/sha256-digest.py is explicitly used and the comparison is described.

assertion_results.text: The response confirms the digests match and states it will proceed silently without prompting the user — no additional latency for the happy path
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 61: "**Result: Proceed silently.** No user prompt is needed, no warning is emitted, and no additional latency is introduced. Implementation continues to Step 2." plan.md line 57: "Compare hex digests: match confirmed. Proceed silently."

assertion_results.text: The response does not alert the user or pause execution due to the digest check — it proceeds directly to subsequent steps
assertion_results.evidence: digest-match.md line 61 explicitly states: "No user prompt is needed, no warning is emitted, and no additional latency is introduced. Implementation continues to Step 2." plan.md flows from Step 1.5 (line 47-57) directly to Step 2 (line 59) with no user interaction. The mismatch scenario is only described as an alternative outcome (digest-match.md lines 64-72), clearly separated and not triggered in this case.
